findings.title: Supabase RLS policies grant unrestricted anonymous CRUD on licenses table
findings.description: RLS policy grants SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on licenses table to anon users with USING (true) / WITH CHECK (true). teacher_profiles and student_profiles also allow anonymous INSERT.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Extract NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and anon key from JS bundle. 2. Use curl or Supabase JS client to directly query/modify licenses table. 3. Create fake licenses, modify seat counts, delete real licenses. 4. Bypass entire licensing system.
findings.impact: Complete bypass of licensing/payment system. Arbitrary data manipulation.
findings.recommendation: Remove anon full-access policies. Use Supabase Edge Functions with service-role key for admin operations. Restrict RLS to authenticated users with role checks.

findings.title: Admin PIN hardcoded as NEXT_PUBLIC_ env var (default '1234'), exposed in JS bundle
findings.description: ADMIN_PIN uses NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix, embedding it in client-side JS. Default is '1234'. Auth state stored as sessionStorage flag '1', trivially bypassable.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Open DevTools, search JS bundle for ADMIN_PIN or '1234'. 2. Or simply run sessionStorage.setItem('phonics_admin_auth', '1') in console. 3. Full admin access without knowing PIN.
findings.impact: Unauthorized admin access to license management, student data, school records.
findings.recommendation: Replace with Supabase Auth admin role. Move admin operations to server-side (Edge Functions).

findings.title: Math.random() used for class codes and license keys (not cryptographically secure)
findings.description: Class codes generated with Math.random(). 6 chars from 32-char alphabet (~1B combinations). Predictable PRNG.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Attacker enumerates class codes by exploiting Math.random() predictability. 2. Joins arbitrary teacher classrooms. 3. Injects fake student data.
findings.impact: Unauthorized access to teacher classrooms, data poisoning.

findings.title: No rate limiting or lockout on admin PIN entry
findings.description: Admin PIN brute-force is trivial. All 10,000 4-digit PINs can be tried in under a second since check is client-side.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Automated script tries PINs 0000-9999. 2. Client-side check means no network delay. 3. Admin access in milliseconds.
findings.recommendation: Move auth server-side with rate limiting and lockout.

findings.title: Android allowBackup=true enables local data extraction
findings.description: android:allowBackup='true' allows ADB backup of app data including student IDs, class codes, learning progress.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Gain physical access to school device. 2. Run adb backup. 3. Extract student data.
findings.impact: Student data exposure on shared school devices.
findings.recommendation: Set android:allowBackup='false' or use restrictive backup rules XML.

findings.title: No Content-Security-Policy or security headers configured
findings.description: No CSP, X-Frame-Options, or X-Content-Type-Options headers. Static export means these must be set at hosting layer.
findings.exploit_scenario: Any XSS vulnerability could execute arbitrary scripts and exfiltrate IndexedDB data.
findings.impact: No defense-in-depth against XSS. Clickjacking possible.
findings.recommendation: Configure security headers at CDN/hosting level. Add CSP with nonce-based inline script policy.

findings.title: Children's privacy compliance gap (COPPA/GDPR/Korean PIPA)
findings.description: No parental consent mechanism, privacy policy, or data deletion workflow. App collects nicknames, learning data, device IDs, and requests microphone access for children ages 6-10.
findings.exploit_scenario: Regulatory audit reveals non-compliance. School district contract violation.
findings.impact: Legal liability, contract loss with schools.
findings.recommendation: Add privacy policy, parental consent flow, and data deletion mechanism.

findings.title: Client-reported learning data has no server-side validation
findings.description: syncLessonToCloud() sends self-reported score_percent and word_results. No server-side validation. Teachers see potentially fabricated data.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Modify client to send score_percent: 100 for all lessons. 2. Teacher dashboard shows perfect scores. 3. School makes decisions based on false data.
findings.impact: Data integrity compromise. False educational reporting to schools.
findings.recommendation: Add server-side score validation via Supabase Edge Function.

findings.title: jsPDF <=4.2.0 has critical PDF Object Injection vulnerability
findings.description: GHSA-7x6v-j9x4-qf24 (PDF Object Injection) and GHSA-wfv2-pwc8-crg5 (HTML Injection). Used for report PDF export.
findings.exploit_scenario: If user-controlled data flows into PDF FreeText annotations, arbitrary PDF objects can be injected.
findings.impact: Potential PDF-based attacks on report viewers.
findings.recommendation: Run npm audit fix to update jsPDF.

findings.title: Client-side activation gate trivially bypassable via localStorage
findings.description: Setting localStorage.setItem('phonics_device_activated', 'anything') bypasses the license/activation check completely.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Open DevTools console. 2. Set localStorage value. 3. Full app access without valid license.
findings.impact: License system bypass. Unauthorized app usage.
findings.recommendation: Validate activation status server-side via Supabase.
